To submit new or missing information, or if you have further suggestions, please don't hesitate to reach out via Discord or Leaked.cx (@WRLDOverlord). Your input is valuable in improving the Tracker, thank you for your contribution. Join The Uzi Tracker Discord Server → discord.gg/spreadsheet

PLEASE BE ADVISED THE TRACKER IS CONSTANTLY UNDER DEVELOPMENT AND CHANGING. I RECOMMEND ZOOMING OUT YOUR WEB PAGE TO 75% FOR EASIER VISIBILITY, AND SEARCHING FOR SONGS USING THE COMMANDS: ‘⌘+F’ (MacOs) OR ‘CTRL + F’ (Windows).

1017 vs. TW7Secure the Bag
Choker
Gucci Mane & Lil Uzi Vert
GLOhanKesha Lee, Roark Bailey & UnknownReleased on SoundCloud as Track #7 on '1017 vs. The World'. The Track was originally titled 'Choker'. Track samples the 'Clock Tower' level theme by Hidenori Shoji and Haruyoshi Tomita from the video game Super Monkey Ball 2. Instrumental was released to Streaming Platforms as Track #7 on GLOhan Beats' 'No Favors'. The track's instrumental was deconstructed by producer GLOhan in a YouTube video.ChokerInstrumental (1): Secure the Bag

Want It That Way | Yellow Queen		Lil Uzi Vert & WanMorFelipe Spain, Supah Mario & Yung LanBrendan Morawski, Chris Athens, Jaycen Joshua & Kesha LeeOriginally released on SoundCloud as a single by Lil Uzi Vert, then was later reissued as Track #18 on Lil Uzi Vert's 'Eternal Atake'. The track's instrumental was partially deconstructed by producer Felipe Spain in a YouTube video, in which he shows the guitar melody for the track.N/AN/AUzi's Vocals

P!NK15Just Wanna Rock
This Ain't What You Want		Lil Uzi VertMcVertt & SyntheticAbdulhafeez Yussuf, Benjamin Thomas, Colin Leonard, Don Cannon & Frank HollandOriginally released on Streaming Platforms as the first lead single for 'Pink Tape', then it was reissued as Track #15 when the album released. P!NK: Level 42Just Wanna Rock
This Ain't What You Want		Lil Uzi VertMcVertt & SyntheticAbdulhafeez Yussuf, Benjamin Thomas, Colin Leonard, Don Cannon & Frank HollandOriginally released on Streaming Platforms as the first lead single for 'Pink Tape', then it was reissued as Track #15 when the album released, then was later reissued a second time as Track #2 on 'Pink Tape: Level 4'. This serves as a post-album EP of re-released tracks from the album, 'Pink Tape'. The track was originally titled 'This Ain't What You Want'. The track was recorded in 20 minutes according to Benjamin Thomas, directly after performing at the 2022. Made In America festival. Track was recorded using a Mobile Recording Setup with Benjamin Thomas at one of Lil Uzi Vert's friend's apartment in New York. The track's instrumental was deconstructed by producers Synthetic and McVertt in a YouTube video.N/AInstrumental: @iamsynthetic_ @mcvertt - higher power 145 bpm

YouTube1Mood
Diamonds | Mess Up My Mood | No Feelings		Lil Uzi Vert, TM88 & SouthsideTM88, Southside & Supah MarioAlex Tumay & Kesha LeeReleased on YouTube as a WorldStarHipHop exclusive and on TM88's SoundCloud, after not being cleared for release to Streaming Platforms. At one point, the Track was available on TM88's website. Track began as a 808 Mafia single featuring Lil Uzi Vert. Track was alternately titled 'No Feelings' for the EDM Remix of the Track. The track faced controversy after its release as the track didn't feature Supah Mario as a credited artist, who had a majority production role. After taking too long to release, Southside enlisted the help of Marshmello for an EDM remix of the track, yet fans favored the release of the original track.Lil Uzi - Mood - TumayMix_05-01Supah Mario's Demo File: Lil Uzi Vert (Mood Instrumental) By.
